assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_043",
  "is_optimal": false,
  "approve": true,
  "feedback": "The fix correctly adds nosuid to /etc/fstab for /boot/efi, but lacks validation and robustness improvements.",
  "concerns": [
    "Potential syntax error in /etc/fstab could prevent boot; requires careful validation.",
    "Reliance on UUID may break if partition is recreated; using LABEL or PARTUUID is more stable.",
    "No verification step performed (e.g., mount -o remount or syntax check).",
    "Missing guidance on testing in staging or using systemd-fstab-generator compatibility."
  ],
  "suggested_improvements": [
    "Validate the edited line with 'grep -E' or 'cat /etc/fstab | grep -v '^#' | awk '$2==\"/boot/efi\"{print}' | wc -l' before applying; use 'mount -o remount /boot/efi' to test.",
    "Prefer using a stable identifier like LABEL=boot-efi or PARTUUID in fstab to avoid breakage on UUID changes.",
    "Add the option via a configuration management tool (e.g., Ansible) to ensure persistence across updates.",
    "Document the change in system documentation and include it in CI pipeline checks."
  ],
  "security_score": 9,
  "best_practices_followed": false
}
